source: WSET L3 Specification exam guidance + official marking key structure

general_principles: All questions based on published learning outcomes; no material outside specification
Marks indicated on exam paper per question/part

question_structure_patterns.Q1_Q2_pattern: Dual RA1+RA2 questions; typically: (a) identify/describe a natural factor [RA1], (b) explain viticultural/vinification implication [RA1], (c) apply to a specific wine/region [RA2], (d) service/recommendation scenario [RA5 minor]
question_structure_patterns.Q3_Q4_pattern: Cross-RA questions covering RA3 (sparkling) or RA4 (fortified) with RA1 application; include RA5 block 1 component (service/recommendation)

mark_earning_principles.description_mark: Give characteristic – 1 mark per valid characteristic
mark_earning_principles.explanation_mark: Give cause-and-effect reasoning – typically worth 1-2 marks per valid causal link
mark_earning_principles.application_mark: Apply RA1 knowledge to RA2 context – requires both fact AND regional link

sample_question_analysis.question_stem: La espaldera es un sistema de conducción con empalizamiento que se utiliza a menudo para el cultivo de la Chardonnay en los climas frescos. Explique por qué un productor de uva optaría por utilizar este sistema.
expected_response_elements: VSP system exposes leaves and grapes to sunlight (identification)
Maximises photosynthesis in limited-sun cool climate (explanation)
Improves canopy ventilation reducing disease risk (additional benefit)
sample_question_analysis.note: Each causal link earns a mark; naming the system alone earns nothing

example_analysis.stem: ¿Cuál es la principal variedad de uva en el vino de mezcla Rosé d'Anjou?
¿Cuál de los siguientes garantizará la estabilidad microbiológica de un vino?

distractor_patterns: Other varieties from same region (plausible regional alternatives)
Correct process for different wine type
True fact but not the best answer

conclusion_markers: drink now but has potential for ageing
